tools.name: find_nearest_toilets
tools.description: Find the nearest PUBLIC TOILETS to a location (from the National Public Toilet Map). Use this for questions like 'where's the closest toilet to me', 'nearest public toilet', 'is there an RV dump point near here', or 'closest accessible toilet'. Returns toilets sorted by distance with name, address,

tools.name: search_campsites
tools.description:
Search for campsites, caravan parks, or accommodation by location, features, and other criteria. Use this when the user asks to find a place to stay, camp, or explore. Returns a list of matching entities with names, locations, ratings, features, and images.

COVERAGE: Australia (default), the United

tools.name: get_campsite_details
tools.description: Get full details for a specific campsite or accommodation by its ID. Returns description, all features/amenities, address, photos, rating, accommodation types offered, and booking information.

tools.name: get_regions
tools.description: List available camping/accommodation regions, optionally filtered by state. Returns region names with entity counts. Use this when the user wants to browse by area or doesn't have a specific location.

tools.name: get_features
tools.description: Get the full list of available features/amenities that campsites can have. Use this to help users understand what filters are available or to clarify what a feature means.

tools.name: get_accommodation_types
tools.description: List all accommodation types offered by a specific entity (e.g. powered sites, unpowered sites, cabins, hotel rooms). Returns pricing, capacity, amenities, and vehicle access info.

tools.name: search_products
tools.description: Search for camping gear, outdoor products, and accessories available in the Camping Australia store. Use this when a user asks about gear, equipment, products, or what to buy/bring.

tools.name: recommend_gear
tools.description: Recommend relevant products and gear for a specific campsite based on its features and type. For example, a campsite with fishing will return fishing gear; a 4WD-access campsite will return recovery equipment. Use this when a user asks what to bring or buy for a particular campsite.

tools.name: get_weather_forecast
tools.description:
Get a 14-day weather forecast for a campsite or location. Use this when the user asks about weather, temperature, rain, wind, or UV conditions at a campsite or destination.

You can provide EITHER:
- campsite_name: The name (or partial name) of a campsite to   look up its GPS coordinates automatical

tools.name: bk_search_operators
tools.description: Find LIVE-BOOKABLE accommodation operators near a location via the Bookeasy booking platform (real-time inventory). Use this when the user wants to actually BOOK and needs live availability/pricing — this is DIFFERENT from search_campsites (our own directory). Returns operator_id values that the oth

tools.name: bk_operator_availability
tools.description: Get LIVE products, pricing and availability for a Bookeasy operator for specific dates. Returns bookable products each with a product_id, price, max_guests and an available flag. Call this (using an operator_id from bk_search_operators) before bk_cart_add.

tools.name: bk_cart_add
tools.description:
Add a product to the Bookeasy booking cart, OR answer a booking question. This is SERVER-DRIVEN and stateful:
- First add: omit session_key to start a new cart; pass operator_id, product_id, check_in, check_out, adults/children, and quoted_price (the price from bk_operator_availability).
- The respo

tools.name: bk_cart_checkout
tools.description:
Finalise the Bookeasy cart and get the SINGLE checkout URL the user uses to complete payment. TWO-STEP, human-in-the-loop:
1. Call WITHOUT confirm — this returns the booking summary + total price and NO payment link. Show the traveller the full summary (property, dates, guests, total) and ask them t

tools.name: bk_cart_guest
tools.description: Attach the lead guest's contact details to the Bookeasy cart. Bookeasy requires a name and email to issue a booking. For a signed-in customer, any of first_name / surname / email you omit are prefilled from their account — so you can call this with just the session_key and confirm the prefilled deta

tools.name: bk_cart_promo
tools.description: Apply (or remove) a promo / discount code on the Bookeasy cart. Use when the user gives a promo code; call before bk_cart_checkout so the discounted total shows. Set remove=true to take a code off.

tools.name: bk_search_content
tools.description: Search curated tourism content (attractions, things to do, local experiences, events) via Bookeasy. Use this for 'what's on / things to do near X' style questions — this is content we do NOT have in our own directory. NOT for campsite/accommodation booking (use the other bk_ tools for that).

tools.name: search_campervans
tools.description: Search live campervan / motorhome hire availability and pricing for a pickup city and travel dates (via DriveAway). Use this when the user wants to RENT a campervan, motorhome or RV (NOT the same as search_campsites, which finds places to camp). Returns matching vehicles with brand, name, category,

tools.name: get_campervan_booking_link
tools.description: Get the DriveAway booking deep-link to complete a campervan / motorhome hire for a pickup city and dates. The user finishes the booking on DriveAway's site (we don't take payment). Use this to hand the user a 'book now' link once they've chosen dates — no search needed. Returns a single booking_url.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "search_campsites" is 1645 chars, far longer than this server's median (304). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Search for campsites, caravan parks, or accommodation by location, features, and other criteria. Use this when the user asks to find a place to stay, camp, or e…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "search_campsites" stacks urgency/override words (important, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Search for campsites, caravan parks, or accommodation by location, features, and other criteria. Use this when the user asks to find a place to stay, camp, or e…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "bk_cart_add" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Add a product to the Bookeasy booking cart, OR answer a booking question. This is SERVER-DRIVEN and stateful: - First add: omit session_key to start a new cart;…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: bk_cart_checkout · Private data: bk_cart_guest · Public sink: bk_cart_guest · Destructive: bk_cart_promo.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: bk_cart_checkout · Private data: bk_cart_guest · Public sink: bk_cart_guest · Destructive: bk_cart_promo

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
